name: Souhaib Ben Taieb
bio: Associate Professor and Associate Department Chair of Statistics and Data Science at MBZUAI. He leads the AIM for Scale AI for Weather Forecasting project and works on AI for operational weather prediction in low- and middle-income countries.

name: Salman Khan
bio: Associate Professor of Computer Vision at MBZUAI. His work spans climate resilience, Earth observation, vision-language models, continual learning, and geospatial AI.

name: Lily Burns
bio: Senior Advisor for the Undergraduate Program at MBZUAI with deep experience in higher education operations, security, policy, and international partnerships.

name: Mohammed Irfan
bio: PhD student focused on personalized AI and wearable technologies, with an interest in making AI feel creative, human-centered, and useful in daily life.

name: Akbobek Abilkaiyrkyzy
bio: Project Manager leading AI and climate initiatives at MBZUAI, with a multidisciplinary background across machine learning, logistics, energy transition, and industrial automation.

name: Bilal Ashfaq
bio: Master's student in Statistics and Data Science researching AI agent security and adaptive defense mechanisms. He brings experience in software engineering, mentorship, and technical instruction.

name: Nicolas Mauricio Cuadrado Avila
bio: PhD candidate in Machine Learning whose research includes efficient machine learning, model pruning and compression, reinforcement learning, and AI for sustainable energy systems.

name: Aya Laajil
bio: Research Engineer in the Statistics and Data Science Department working on AI-driven weather forecasting for Africa, with interests in machine learning, probabilistic calibration, and applied statistics.

name: Nokhutula Madekufamba
bio: Master's student in Big Data Science and Mastercard Foundation Scholar focused on data science, machine learning, and responsible AI for real-world impact.

name: Caivil Ndobela
bio: Bioinformatics scholar with research, teaching, and mentorship experience across Southern Africa. He has supported research methodology, data analysis, and technical training for postgraduate students.
